LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA CONTRACTORS STATE LICENSE BOARD'S DEMURRER TO THE PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS OVERRULED. THE COURT IS CHARACTERIZING THE DEMURRER AS TO FEES AND DAMAGES AS A MOTION TO STRIKE. THE MOTION TO STRIKE AS TO SECTION 800 FEES IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE MOTION TO STRIKE AS TO CCP 1021.5 FEES IS DENIED. THE MOTION TO STRIKE AS TO DAMAGES IS GRANTED WITH THIRTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. COUNSEL FOR THE PETITONER IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER REFLECTING THE COURT'S RULING AND SHOW COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Dec-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Shona Armstrong of Harper & Armstrong, LLP telephone number 510-420-8455 for Petitioner Stonewood Kitchen and Bath.
Joshua Room of Office of the Attorney General telephone number415-703-1299 for Respondent California Contractors State License Board.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court modified its tentative ruling.
Respondent California Contractors State License Board's demurrer to the Petition for writ of mandate is overruled. The Court is characterizing the demurrer as to fees and damages as a motion to strike. The motion to strike as to Section 800 fees is granted without leave to amend. The motion to strike as to CCP 1021.5 fees is denied. The motion to strike as to damages is granted with thirty days leave to amend. Counsel for the Petitoner is to prepare a form of order reflecting the Court's ruling and show compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
